title: SACRAMENTO - In an effort to combat the detrimental effects of climate change, legislation to expand opportunities for marine restoration work along the California Coast was signed
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Marine life restoration plays an essential role in mitigating the devastating effects of climate change and human impacts.",
    "Kelp restoration ensures that oceans retain the critical benefits of carbon retention and provide food and habitats for over 800 species.",
    "90% of bull kelp has been lost due to rising sea temperatures in Northern California.",
    "Restoration is a critical tool to respond to environmental changes.",
    "The MMAIA establishes six classifications designating managed areas in marine and estuarine environments.",
    "AB 63 will update MMAIA to include restoration as a permitted activity in marine conservation areas.",
    "Culling superabundant sea urchins will restore denuded kelp forests and protect biodiversity.",
    "AB 63 will support local and regional projects necessary for restoring State Marine Protected Areas."
  ]
}
```
